disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Rapid onset of cramping abdominal pain, bloating and profuse diarrhoea within about 30 minutes of eating a previously tolerated food, on a background of recurrent milder similar episodes.
trigger_question_examples: Can you tell me exactly when this started and what you were doing?
How soon after eating did the symptoms come on?
Have you had episodes like this before, even milder ones?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It kicked off maybe half an hour after dinner. I've eaten that food fine before. I do get flare-ups like this now and then, but tonight is way worse.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Cutaneous flushing (face and chest go red and hot) accompanying the gastrointestinal episode.
trigger_question_examples: Do you notice any flushing, or your skin going red and hot?
Any skin changes — redness, hives, itching — with these episodes?
Does your face or chest feel warm or look flushed when this happens?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, now that you ask — my face and chest go really red and hot when it's bad. I didn't think that was connected.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I haven't really looked at my skin, I've been stuck on the toilet.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Presyncope / near-fainting on standing with ongoing fluid loss, in a patient who lives alone.
trigger_question_examples: Are you feeling dizzy or faint, especially when you stand up?
Do you feel like you might pass out?
Is anyone with you at home right now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: When I stand up I feel like I'm going to black out, I have to grab the wall. And no, there's no one here with me tonight.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No blood in the stool and no vomiting of blood (pertinent negative screened this encounter).
trigger_question_examples: Have you seen any blood in the diarrhoea, or is it black and tarry?
Any blood when you've been sick, or blood in your poo?
Is there any red or dark blood coming from your bowels?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, no blood that I've seen — it's just watery, no red or black in it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Still passing urine — reduced but present; used to gauge hydration and screen for developing anuria/AKI.
trigger_question_examples: When did you last pass urine, and how much?
Are you still weeing normally, or has it dropped off?
Have you been able to pass urine in the last few hours?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I did go a little while ago but not much, and it was pretty dark. I haven't needed to go much tonight.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No throat tightness, lip or tongue swelling, wheeze or difficulty breathing (anaphylaxis screen negative on obtainable data).
trigger_question_examples: Is your throat tight, or are your lips or tongue swelling at all?
Are you having any trouble breathing, or any wheeze?
Any tightness in your chest or throat, or trouble swallowing?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, my breathing's okay and my throat feels normal — it's all my stomach and the dizziness.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known background of food-triggered reactions with prior clinician mention of mast cell involvement; not currently on any preventive/mast-cell-directed medication.
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any diagnosed conditions or ongoing problems with food reactions?
Has a doctor ever told you what's behind these flare-ups?
Are you taking any regular medications or preventers for this?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I react to a lot of foods and a doctor once said my mast cells might be involved. I'm not on any regular medication for it at the moment.
